message:
fix: drain steering messages after stop event, fix spill file leak, and simplify SSE UTF-8 handling

Steering message delivery (agent.rs):
- Call drain_steering_messages() after the stop event is processed so that
  any steering messages queued during the turn are emitted as MessageStart/
  MessageEnd events and appended to the turn's new_messages list; previously
  these messages were silently dropped when a stop event ended the turn

Bash output spill file handling (rpc.rs, tools.rs):
- When bash output exceeds the hard limit, close the spill file by setting
  temp_file to None instead of calling abandon_spill_file which deletes it;
  this releases the file descriptor without losing the partial output that
  was already written, which may be needed for diagnostics
- Applies the same fix in both the RPC and direct tool code paths

SSE UTF-8 stream processing (sse.rs):
- Remove first_error tracking from process_chunk_without_utf8_tail and
  process_chunk_with_utf8_tail; invalid UTF-8 sequences are now silently
  skipped (the valid portions are still fed to the parser) instead of
  returning an error after processing completes
- This matches the resilient behavior expected for SSE streams from LLM
  providers where occasional invalid bytes should not abort the entire
  stream

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
